steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank is an introductory prepositional phrase (“With over two millennia of climate data in its tree”). The right side (after the blank) contains an independent clause with a subject (the noun phrase beginning with the blank's second word) and a main verb (“can tell”), with a parenthetical attribution interrupting in the middle.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A comma separates an introductory phrase from the main independent clause. Option A (rings, a) correctly places the comma after the introductory phrase and provides the article “a” needed for the subject. Options B, C, and D either create a sentence fragment, form an illogical compound construction, or misuse punctuation meant for independent clauses. That matches choice A — A is correct.
